title: Registers
intro: Yanks and deletes normally share one clipboard, so a delete overwrites what you yanked. Named registers fix that: prefix with `"` and a letter to pick a specific register — `"ayy` yanks a line into register a, `"ap` pastes it back, untouched by any deletes in between.
summary: Named clipboards for yanks and deletes.

exercises.instruction: Yank the first line into register a with `"ayy`, move down twice with `jj`, then paste it below with `"ap`.

goal.buffer:
HEADER
line b
line c
HEADER

hints: `"a` selects register a for the very next yank or paste.

exercises.instruction: Registers survive deletes. Yank line 1 into register a with `"ayy`, delete line 2 with `dd`, then paste register a below it with `"ap`.
hints: `dd` overwrites the default register but leaves register a alone.

exercises.instruction: Register `0` always holds your last yank, even after a delete. Yank line 1 with `yy`, move down and delete `trash` with `jdd`, then paste the yank with `"0p`. (Plain `p` would paste `trash` instead!)
hints: `yy` fills register 0; the later `dd` fills the default register but not 0, so `"0p` still pastes `keep`.

exercises.instruction: Keep a yank safe across a delete: yank the first line with `"ayy`, delete the `old` line with `j` `dd`, then paste register a below with `"ap`.
hints: `dd` only touches the default register — register a still holds `MARKER`.

exercises.instruction: Stamp the header twice: `"ayy` the first line, then `j` `"ap`, then `j` `"ap` again.
hints: A named register keeps its contents — you can `"ap` as many times as you like.
